title: protorhus thouarsii
intro: Protorhus thouarsii, also known as the South African red stinkwood, is a tree species native to the tropical regions of Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Protorhus thouarsii include the potential treatment of various conditions such as diarrhea, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Protorhus thouarsii may include extracts or infusions of the tree's bark, leaves, or roots, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical creams.
